ERC20InsufficientAllowance(address,uint2.details: Indicates a failure with the `spender`’s `allowance`. Used in transfers.

params.allowance: Amount of tokens a `spender` is allowed to operate with.
params.needed: Minimum amount required to perform a transfer.
params.spender: Address that may be allowed to operate on tokens without being their owner.

ERC20InsufficientBalance(address,uint256.details: Indicates an error related to the current `balance` of a `sender`. Used in transfers.

params.balance: Current balance for the interacting account.
params.needed: Minimum amount required to perform a transfer.
params.sender: Address whose tokens are being transferred.

ERC20InvalidApprover(address).details: Indicates a failure with the `approver` of a token to be approved. Used in approvals.

ERC20InvalidReceiver(address).details: Indicates a failure with the token `receiver`. Used in transfers.

params.receiver: Address to which tokens are being transferred.

ERC20InvalidSender(address).details: Indicates a failure with the token `sender`. Used in transfers.

params.sender: Address whose tokens are being transferred.

ERC20InvalidSpender(address).details: Indicates a failure with the `spender` to be approved. Used in approvals.

params.spender: Address that may be allowed to operate on tokens without being their owner.

ERC4626ExceededMaxDeposit(address,uint25.details: Attempted to deposit more assets than the max amount for `receiver`.

ERC4626ExceededMaxMint(address,uint256,u.details: Attempted to mint more shares than the max amount for `receiver`.

ERC4626ExceededMaxRedeem(address,uint256.details: Attempted to redeem more shares than the max amount for `owner`.

ERC4626ExceededMaxWithdraw(address,uint2.details: Attempted to withdraw more assets than the max amount for `owner`.

EnforcedPause().details: The operation failed because the contract is paused.

ExpectedPause().details: The operation failed because the contract is not paused.

OwnableInvalidOwner(address).details: The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)

OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address).details: The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation.

SafeERC20FailedOperation(address).details: An operation with an ERC-20 token failed.

Approval(address,address,uint256).details: Emitted when the allowance of a `spender` for an `owner` is set by a call to {approve}. `value` is the new allowance.

Initialized(uint64).details: Triggered when the contract has been initialized or reinitialized.

Paused(address).details: Emitted when the pause is triggered by `account`.

Transfer(address,address,uint256).details: Emitted when `value` tokens are moved from one account (`from`) to another (`to`). Note that `value` may be zero.

Unpaused(address).details: Emitted when the pause is lifted by `account`.

allowance(address,address).details: Returns the remaining number of tokens that `spender` will be allowed to spend on behalf of `owner` through {transferFrom}. This is zero by default. This value changes when {approve} or {transferFrom} are called.

approve(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-approve}. NOTE: If `value` is the maximum `uint256`, the allowance is not updated on `transferFrom`. This is semantically equivalent to an infinite approval. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address.

asset().details: Returns the address of the underlying token used for the Vault for accounting, depositing, and withdrawing. - MUST be an ERC-20 token contract. - MUST NOT revert.

balanceOf(address).details: Returns the value of tokens owned by `account`.

returns._1: fullyConsumedCount Number of fully consumable records
returns._2: partialConsumedAmount Partially consumable amount in the last record

canWithdrawalAmount(address,uint256).details: A record becomes claimable when its waiting period has elapsed and the cumulative available amount clears its queued baseline

convertToAssets(uint256).details: Returns the amount of assets that the Vault would exchange for the amount of shares provided, in an ideal scenario where all the conditions are met. - MUST NOT be inclusive of any fees that are charged against assets in the Vault. - MUST NOT show any variations depending on the caller. - MUST NOT reflect slippage or other on-chain conditions, when performing the actual exchange. - MUST NOT revert. NOTE: This calculation MAY NOT reflect the “per-user” price-per-share, and instead should reflect the “average-user’s” price-per-share, meaning what the average user should expect to see when exchanging to and from.

convertToShares(uint256).details: Returns the amount of shares that the Vault would exchange for the amount of assets provided, in an ideal scenario where all the conditions are met. - MUST NOT be inclusive of any fees that are charged against assets in the Vault. - MUST NOT show any variations depending on the caller. - MUST NOT reflect slippage or other on-chain conditions, when performing the actual exchange. - MUST NOT revert. NOTE: This calculation MAY NOT reflect the “per-user” price-per-share, and instead should reflect the “average-user’s” price-per-share, meaning what the average user should expect to see when exchanging to and from.

decimals().details: Decimals are computed by adding the decimal offset on top of the underlying asset's decimals. This "original" value is cached during construction of the vault contract. If this read operation fails (e.g., the asset has not been created yet), a default of 18 is used to represent the underlying asset's decimals. See {IERC20Metadata-decimals}.

deposit(uint256,address).details: In the current mainnet phase (Oracle = 1:1), this path mints stPROS 1:1

maxDeposit(address).details: Returns the maximum amount of the underlying asset that can be deposited into the Vault for the receiver, through a deposit call. - MUST return a limited value if receiver is subject to some deposit limit. - MUST return 2 ** 256 - 1 if there is no limit on the maximum amount of assets that may be deposited. - MUST NOT revert.

maxMint(address).details: Returns the maximum amount of the Vault shares that can be minted for the receiver, through a mint call. - MUST return a limited value if receiver is subject to some mint limit. - MUST return 2 ** 256 - 1 if there is no limit on the maximum amount of shares that may be minted. - MUST NOT revert.

maxRedeem(address).details: Returns the maximum amount of Vault shares that can be redeemed from the owner balance in the Vault, through a redeem call. - MUST return a limited value if owner is subject to some withdrawal limit or timelock. - MUST return balanceOf(owner) if owner is not subject to any withdrawal limit or timelock. - MUST NOT revert.

maxWithdraw(address).details: Returns the maximum amount of the underlying asset that can be withdrawn from the owner balance in the Vault, through a withdraw call. - MUST return a limited value if owner is subject to some withdrawal limit or timelock. - MUST NOT revert.

mint(uint256,address).details: In the current mainnet phase (Oracle = 1:1), this path back-solves assets 1:1

name().details: Returns the name of the token.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

previewDeposit(uint256).details: Allows an on-chain or off-chain user to simulate the effects of their deposit at the current block, given current on-chain conditions. - MUST return as close to and no more than the exact amount of Vault shares that would be minted in a deposit   call in the same transaction. I.e. deposit should return the same or more shares as previewDeposit if called   in the same transaction. - MUST NOT account for deposit limits like those returned from maxDeposit and should always act as though the   deposit would be accepted, regardless if the user has enough tokens approved, etc. - MUST be inclusive of deposit fees. Integrators should be aware of the existence of deposit fees. - MUST NOT revert. NOTE: any unfavorable discrepancy between convertToShares and previewDeposit SHOULD be considered slippage in share price or some other type of condition, meaning the depositor will lose assets by depositing.

previewMint(uint256).details: Allows an on-chain or off-chain user to simulate the effects of their mint at the current block, given current on-chain conditions. - MUST return as close to and no fewer than the exact amount of assets that would be deposited in a mint call   in the same transaction. I.e. mint should return the same or fewer assets as previewMint if called in the   same transaction. - MUST NOT account for mint limits like those returned from maxMint and should always act as though the mint   would be accepted, regardless if the user has enough tokens approved, etc. - MUST be inclusive of deposit fees. Integrators should be aware of the existence of deposit fees. - MUST NOT revert. NOTE: any unfavorable discrepancy between convertToAssets and previewMint SHOULD be considered slippage in share price or some other type of condition, meaning the depositor will lose assets by minting.

previewRedeem(uint256).details: Allows an on-chain or off-chain user to simulate the effects of their redemption at the current block, given current on-chain conditions. - MUST return as close to and no more than the exact amount of assets that would be withdrawn in a redeem call   in the same transaction. I.e. redeem should return the same or more assets as previewRedeem if called in the   same transaction. - MUST NOT account for redemption limits like those returned from maxRedeem and should always act as though the   redemption would be accepted, regardless if the user has enough shares, etc. - MUST be inclusive of withdrawal fees. Integrators should be aware of the existence of withdrawal fees. - MUST NOT revert. NOTE: any unfavorable discrepancy between convertToAssets and previewRedeem SHOULD be considered slippage in share price or some other type of condition, meaning the depositor will lose assets by redeeming.

previewWithdraw(uint256).details: Allows an on-chain or off-chain user to simulate the effects of their withdrawal at the current block, given current on-chain conditions. - MUST return as close to and no fewer than the exact amount of Vault shares that would be burned in a withdraw   call in the same transaction. I.e. withdraw should return the same or fewer shares as previewWithdraw if   called   in the same transaction. - MUST NOT account for withdrawal limits like those returned from maxWithdraw and should always act as though   the withdrawal would be accepted, regardless if the user has enough shares, etc. - MUST be inclusive of withdrawal fees. Integrators should be aware of the existence of withdrawal fees. - MUST NOT revert. NOTE: any unfavorable discrepancy between convertToShares and previewWithdraw SHOULD be considered slippage in share price or some other type of condition, meaning the depositor will lose assets by depositing.

redeem(uint256,address,address).details: In the current mainnet phase (Oracle = 1:1), `shares` map to equal `assets`

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

setUnbondingPeriod(uint256).details: Only affects future requests; historical records keep their snapshots

symbol().details: Returns the symbol of the token, usually a shorter version of the name.

totalSupply().details: Returns the value of tokens in existence.

transfer(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transfer}. Requirements: - `to` cannot be the zero address. - the caller must have a balance of at least `value`.

transferFrom(address,address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transferFrom}. Skips emitting an {Approval} event indicating an allowance update. This is not required by the ERC. See {xref-ERC20-_approve-address-address-uint256-bool-}[_approve]. NOTE: Does not update the allowance if the current allowance is the maximum `uint256`. Requirements: - `from` and `to` cannot be the zero address. - `from` must have a balance of at least `value`. - the caller must have allowance for ``from``'s tokens of at least `value`.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

withdraw(uint256,address,address).details: In the current mainnet phase (Oracle = 1:1), `assets` match the escrowed shares

params.maxRecords: Maximum number of fully consumed records to process; 0 means no limit
Maximum number of fully consumed records to process; 0 means no limit

revert InvalidAddress();\n        }\n        __Ownable_init(owner);\n        __Pausable_init();\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Set the pool info, only callable by bridge\n    /// @param _token The token {\n        poolInfo[_token] = PoolInfo({tokenAmount: _tokenAmount, vTokenAmount: _vTokenAmount});\n        emit SetTokenAmount(_token, _tokenAmount, _vTokenAmount); \n    }\n\n    /// @notice Get vToken by token.\n    /// @param _token The token address\n    /// @param _tokenAmount The token amount\n    /// @param _rounding The rounding mode\n    /// @return The vToken amount\n    function getVTokenAmountByToken(address _token, uint256 _tokenAmount, Math.Rounding _rounding)\n        public\n        view\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        PoolInfo memory pool = /// @param _rounding The rounding mode\n    /// @return The token amount\n    function getTokenAmountByVToken(address _token, uint256 _vTokenAmount, Math.Rounding _rounding)\n        public\n        view\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        PoolInfo memory pool = poolInfo[_token];\n        if (pool.vTokenAmount == 0 || pool.tokenAmount == 0) {\n            return _vTokenAmount;\n        }\n        uint256 tokenAmount = _vTokenAmount.mulDiv(pool.tokenAmount, pool.vTokenAmount, _rounding);\n        return tokenAmount;\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Pause the contract\n    function pause() solidity ^0.8.28;\n\nimport {VToken} from \"./VToken.sol\";\nimport {IWPROS} from \"./IWPROS.sol\";\nimport {IERC20} from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/IERC20.sol\";\nimport {ReentrancyGuardTransient} from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/ReentrancyGuardTransient.sol\";\n\ncontract StPROS is VToken, ReentrancyGuardTransient {\n    /// @custom:oz-upgrades-unsafe-allow constructor\n    constructor() {\n        _disableInitializers();\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Thrown when PROS is not sent\n    error PROSNotSent();\n\n    /// @notice Thrown when PROS transfer failed\n    error TransferFailed();\n    /// @notice Thrown when ETH is sent from non-asset address\n    error OnlyAssetCanSendETH();\n\n    /// @notice Emitted when PROS is received\n    event PROSReceived(address indexed sender, uint256 amount);\n\n    /// @notice Override initialize to include reentrancy guard (for new deployments)\n    function initialize(IERC20 asset, address owner, string memory name, string memory symbol)\n        public\n        initializer\n    {\n        __VToken_init(asset, owner, name, symbol);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Receive PROS from V_PROS withdrawal\n    receive() external payable {\n        if (msg.sender != address(asset())) {\n            revert OnlyAssetCanSendETH();\n        }\n        emit PROSReceived(_msgSender(), msg.value);\n    }\n\n    function depositWithPROS() external payable whenNotPaused nonReentrant returns (uint256) {\n        if (msg.value == 0) {\n            revert PROSNotSent();\n        }\n        // Convert PROS to V_PROS (V_PROS will be sent to this contract)\n        IWPROS(address(asset())).deposit{value: msg.value}();\n\n        uint256 shares = previewDeposit(msg.value);\n        _mint(msg.sender, shares);          // Assets are already held by the contract, so mint shares directly and skip transferFrom.\n        emit Deposit(msg.sender, msg.sender, msg.value, shares);\n        return shares;\n    }\n\n    function withdrawCompleteToPROS() external whenNotPaused nonReentrant returns (uint256) {\n        uint256 amount = super.withdrawComplete(address(this));\n        IWPROS(address(asset())).withdraw(amount);\n        (bool success,) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}(\"\");\n        if (!success) {\n            revert TransferFailed();\n        }\n        return amount;\n    }\n\n    function withdrawCompleteToPROS(uint256 maxRecords) external whenNotPaused nonReentrant returns (uint256) {\n        uint256 amount = super.withdrawComplete(address(this), maxRecords);\n        IWPROS(address(asset())).withdraw(amount);\n        (bool success,) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}(\"\");\n        if (!success) {\n            revert TransferFailed();\n        }\n        return amount;\n    }\n}","keccak256":"0x820ea253dc986b89bc0fcfa265825126e7739068b6d54e110ef12a4f6c370456","license":"GPL-3.0"},"project/contracts/VToken.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0\npragma solidity ^0.8.24;\n\nimport {IERC20} from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/IERC20.sol\";\nimport {ERC20} from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/ERC20.sol\";\nimport {IERC4626} from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/interfaces/IERC4626.sol\";\nimport {SafeERC20} from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/utils/SafeERC20.sol\";\nimport {Math} from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/math/Math.sol\";\nimport {ERC4626Upgradeable} from \"@openzeppelin/contracts-upgradeable/token/ERC20/extensions/ERC4626Upgradeable.sol\";\nimport {OwnableUpgradeable} from \"@openzeppelin/contracts-upgradeable/access/OwnableUpgradeable.sol\";\nimport {PausableUpgradeable} from \"@openzeppelin/contracts-upgradeable/utils/PausableUpgradeable.sol\";\nimport {ERC165Upgradeable} from \"@openzeppelin/contracts-upgradeable/utils/introspection/ERC165Upgradeable.sol\";\nimport {Oracle} from \"./Oracle.sol\";\n\n/**\n * @title VToken\n * @notice Core stPROS vault (ERC-4626) responsible for subscriptions, queued\n * redemptions, and withdrawal completion.\n *\n * Mint flow in the current mainnet phase:\n * 1) Users deposit the underlying PROS asset (or its wrapped form) through\n *    `deposit` / `mint`.\n * 2) Share conversion is provided by {Oracle}; the current mainnet setup uses\n *    a 1:1 ratio (`assets == shares`).\n * 3) The INV-1 invariant must always hold: the internal PROS accounting must\n *    match the total stPROS supply.\n *\n * Redemption flow in the current mainnet phase:\n * 1) Users call `withdraw` / `redeem`, which enqueues the request instead of\n *    paying out immediately.\n * 2) Each queue item snapshots the waiting period (`unbondingPeriod`) at the\n *    time of submission.\n * 3) `withdrawComplete` checks both \"waiting period elapsed\" and \"reserve is\n *    available\".\n * 4) Batched processing via `maxRecords` helps keep gas usage manageable.\n *\n * Safety properties:\n * - INV-1: internal PROS accounting must equal the stPROS total supply.\n * - The queue uses head / tail cursors for O(1) enqueue operations and avoids\n *   shifting entire arrays.\n *\n * Oracle-based mint / redeem conversion:\n * - All ERC-4626 conversions in this contract are delegated to {Oracle}:\n *   - assets -> shares: `oracle.getVTokenAmountByToken(...)`\n *   - shares -> assets: `oracle.getTokenAmountByVToken(...)`\n * - In the current mainnet phase the Oracle is expected to remain configured\n *   at 1:1 (`tokenAmount == vTokenAmount`):\n *   - `deposit` / `mint` behave as 1:1 minting\n *   - `withdraw` / `redeem` behave as 1:1 redemption\n * - If the Oracle is reconfigured away from 1:1 in the future, this contract\n *   automatically follows the new rate without requiring VToken changes.\n */\ncontract VToken is ERC4626Upgradeable, OwnableUpgradeable, PausableUpgradeable, ERC165Upgradeable {\n    using Math for uint256;\n    using SafeERC20 for IERC20;\n\n    /// @custom:oz-upgrades-unsafe-allow constructor\n    constructor() {\n        _disableInitializers();\n    }\n\n    // =================== Type declarations ===================\n    /// @notice A single queued redemption request\n    struct Withdrawal {\n        /// @notice Cumulative queued baseline at submission time\n        uint256 queued;\n        /// @notice Remaining asset amount that has not yet been claimed\n        uint256 pending;\n        /// @notice Submission timestamp\n        uint256 createdAt;\n        /// @notice Waiting period snapshot taken at submission time, in seconds\n        uint256 unbondingPeriod;\n    }\n\n    // =================== State variables ===================\n\n    /// @notice Rate oracle for token <-> vToken conversion\n    /// @dev In the current mainnet phase it is expected to stay at 1:1\n    Oracle public oracle;\n\n    /// @notice Reserve currently available for completion, decreases on claim\n    uint256 public totalCanWithdrawAmount;\n\n    /// @notice Historical total queued amount, monotonically increasing\n    uint256 public queuedWithdrawal;\n\n    /// @notice Historical total completed amount, monotonically increasing\n    uint256 public completedWithdrawal;\n\n    /// @notice Withdraw queue head index per user (inclusive)\n    mapping(address => uint256) public withdrawalHead;\n\n    /// @notice Withdraw queue tail index per user (exclusive)\n    mapping(address => uint256) public withdrawalTail;\n\n    /// @notice Withdraw queue storage per user and index\n    mapping(address => mapping(uint256 => Withdrawal)) internal withdrawals;\n\n    /// @notice Maximum number of outstanding withdrawal records per address\n    uint256 public maxWithdrawCount;\n\n    /// @notice Global waiting period used for new withdrawal requests, in seconds\n    uint256 public unbondingPeriod;\n\n    /// @notice Safe default max queue length per address to avoid a locked withdrawal path\n    uint256 internal constant DEFAULT_MAX_WITHDRAW_COUNT = 5;\n\n    /// @notice Default waiting period set during initialization\n    uint256 internal constant DEFAULT_UNBONDING_PERIOD = 7 days;\n    /// @notice Governance cap on the waiting period to avoid excessive lockups\n    uint256 public constant MAX_UNBONDING = 30 days;\n\n    /// @notice Internal accounting driven only by mint / burn, ignoring direct transfers\n    uint256 internal _tracked;\n\n    // =================== Events ===================\n\n    /// @notice Trigger address update event\n    event TriggerAddressChanged(address indexed oldAddress, address indexed newAddress);\n\n    /// @notice Oracle address update event\n    event OracleChanged(address indexed oldOracle, address indexed newOracle);\n\n    /// @notice Successful withdrawal completion event\n    event WithdrawalCompleted(address indexed caller, address indexed receiver, uint256 tokenAmount);\n\n    /// @notice Max queue length update event\n    event MaxWithdrawCountChanged(uint256 maxWithdrawCount);\n\n    /// @notice Global waiting period update event for future requests only\n    event UnbondingPeriodChanged(uint256 oldUnbondingPeriod, uint256 newUnbondingPeriod);\n\n    // =================== Errors ===================\n\n    /// @notice Outstanding withdrawal records exceed the configured limit\n    error ExceedMaxWithdrawCount(uint256 withdrawCount);\n\n    /// @notice Invalid address parameter such as the zero address\n    error InvalidAddress();\n\n    /// @notice INV-1 is broken: tracked PROS amount != stPROS supply\n    error Inv1Violation(uint256 prosBalance, uint256 stProsSupply);\n    /// @notice Waiting period exceeds the governance cap\n    error UnbondingPeriodTooLong(uint256 value, uint256 maxValue);\n\n    // =================== Modifiers ===================\n\n    /// @notice Validate INV-1 before and after critical entry points\n    modifier checkInv1() {\n        _assertInv1();\n        _;\n        _assertInv1();\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Initialize ERC20 / ERC4626 / access-control modules\n    function __VToken_init(IERC20 _asset, address _owner, string memory _name, string memory _symbol)\n        internal\n        onlyInitializing\n    {\n        __ERC20_init(_name, _symbol);\n        __ERC4626_init(_asset);\n        __Ownable_init(_owner);\n        __Pausable_init();\n        __ERC165_init();\n\n        // Set a safe explicit default so withdrawals don't get disabled by a zero value.\n        maxWithdrawCount = DEFAULT_MAX_WITHDRAW_COUNT;\n        // Set an explicit default waiting period to preserve the intended economics.\n        unbondingPeriod = DEFAULT_UNBONDING_PERIOD;\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Set the oracle address, owner only\n    function setOracle(address _oracle) external onlyOwner {\n      if(_oracle == address(0)) {\n        revert InvalidAddress();\n      }\n        address oldOracle = address(oracle);\n        oracle = Oracle(_oracle);\n        emit OracleChanged(oldOracle, _oracle);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Set the max number of queued records per address, owner only\n    function setMaxWithdrawCount(uint256 _maxWithdrawCount) external onlyOwner {\n        maxWithdrawCount = _maxWithdrawCount;\n        emit MaxWithdrawCountChanged(_maxWithdrawCount);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Set the global waiting period, owner only\n    /// @dev Only affects future requests; historical records keep their snapshots\n    function setUnbondingPeriod(uint256 _unbondingPeriod) external onlyOwner {\n        if (_unbondingPeriod > MAX_UNBONDING) {\n            revert UnbondingPeriodTooLong(_unbondingPeriod, MAX_UNBONDING);\n        }\n        uint256 oldUnbondingPeriod = unbondingPeriod;\n        unbondingPeriod = _unbondingPeriod;\n        emit UnbondingPeriodChanged(oldUnbondingPeriod, _unbondingPeriod);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Pause pausable entry points, owner only\n    function pause() external onlyOwner {\n        _pause();\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Unpause, owner only\n    function unpause() external onlyOwner {\n        _unpause();\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Complete claimable withdrawals, processing all records by default\n    /// @return amount Actual assets claimed\n    function withdrawComplete() public returns (uint256) {\n        return withdrawComplete(msg.sender, type(uint8).max);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Complete claimable withdrawals in batches, paying the caller by default\n    /// @param maxRecords Maximum number of fully consumed records to process; 0 means no limit\n    /// @return amount Actual assets claimed\n    function withdrawComplete(uint256 maxRecords) public returns (uint256) {\n        return withdrawComplete(msg.sender, maxRecords);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Complete claimable withdrawals, processing all records by default\n    /// @param receiver Asset receiver\n    /// @return amount Actual assets claimed\n    function withdrawComplete(address receiver) public returns (uint256) {\n        return withdrawComplete(receiver, type(uint8).max);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Complete claimable withdrawals in batches to control single-call gas\n    /// @param receiver Asset receiver\n    /// @param maxRecords Maximum number of fully consumed records to process; 0 means no limit\n    /// @return amount Actual assets claimed\n    function withdrawComplete(address receiver, uint256 maxRecords) public returns (uint256) {\n        (uint256 totalAvailableAmount, uint256 fullyConsumedCount, uint256 partialConsumedAmount) =\n            canWithdrawalAmount(msg.sender, maxRecords);\n\n        if (totalAvailableAmount == 0) {\n            return 0;\n        }\n\n        // Remove fully consumed records and advance the head cursor.\n        uint256 head = withdrawalHead[msg.sender];\n        for (uint256 i = 0; i < fullyConsumedCount; i++) {\n            delete withdrawals[msg.sender][head + i];\n        }\n        unchecked {\n            head += fullyConsumedCount;\n        }\n        withdrawalHead[msg.sender] = head;\n\n        // Handle the final partially consumed record if any.\n        if (partialConsumedAmount > 0) {\n            Withdrawal storage w = withdrawals[msg.sender][head];\n            unchecked {\n                w.pending -= partialConsumedAmount;\n                w.queued += partialConsumedAmount;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update cumulative accounting and transfer assets.\n        completedWithdrawal += totalAvailableAmount;\n        totalCanWithdrawAmount -= totalAvailableAmount;\n        _burn(address(this), totalAvailableAmount);\n        IERC20(address(asset())).safeTransfer(receiver, totalAvailableAmount);\n        emit WithdrawalCompleted(msg.sender, receiver, totalAvailableAmount);\n        return totalAvailableAmount;\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Preview the currently claimable amount for an address with no record limit\n    /// @return totalAvailableAmount Total claimable amount\n    /// @return fullyConsumedCount Number of fully consumable records\n    /// @return partialConsumedAmount Partially consumable amount in the last record\n    function canWithdrawalAmount(address target) public view returns (uint256, uint256, uint256) {\n        return canWithdrawalAmount(target, type(uint8).max);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Preview the claimable amount for an address with batched processing\n    /// @dev A record becomes claimable when its waiting period has elapsed and the cumulative available amount clears its queued baseline\n    function canWithdrawalAmount(address target, uint256 maxRecords) public view returns (uint256, uint256, uint256) {\n        uint256 totalAvailableAmount = 0;\n        uint256 fullyConsumedCount = 0;\n        uint256 partialConsumedAmount = 0;\n        // Use \"historical completed + current reserve\" so batched and one-shot processing stay consistent.\n        uint256 cumulativeAvailableAmount = completedWithdrawal + totalCanWithdrawAmount;\n        uint256 head = withdrawalHead[target];\n        uint256 tail = withdrawalTail[target];\n        uint256 limit = maxRecords == 0 ? type(uint8).max : maxRecords;\n\n        // Process in submission order: if an earlier record is not unlocked or underfunded, later ones stay blocked too.\n        for (uint256 index = head; index < tail && fullyConsumedCount < limit; index++) {\n            Withdrawal memory w = withdrawals[target][index];\n            uint256 unlockAt = w.createdAt + w.unbondingPeriod;\n            if (block.timestamp < unlockAt) {\n                break;\n            }\n            if (cumulativeAvailableAmount > w.queued) {\n                uint256 currentAvailableAmount = cumulativeAvailableAmount - w.queued;\n                if (currentAvailableAmount < w.pending) {\n                    totalAvailableAmount += currentAvailableAmount;\n                    partialConsumedAmount = currentAvailableAmount;\n                    break;\n                } else {\n                    totalAvailableAmount += w.pending;\n                    fullyConsumedCount += 1;\n                }\n            } else {\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n        return (totalAvailableAmount, fullyConsumedCount, partialConsumedAmount);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Return the current outstanding withdrawal records for an address in queue order\n    function getWithdrawals(address target) public view returns (Withdrawal[] memory) {\n        uint256 head = withdrawalHead[target];\n        uint256 tail = withdrawalTail[target];\n        uint256 length = tail - head;\n        Withdrawal[] memory result = new Withdrawal[](length);\n        for (uint256 i = 0; i < length; i++) {\n            result[i] = withdrawals[target][head + i];\n        }\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    // =================== ERC4626 functions ===================\n    /// @notice ERC4626 total-assets view derived from the oracle and current supply\n    /// @dev Under a 1:1 oracle config, `totalAssets()` equals `totalSupply()`\n    function totalAssets() public view virtual override returns (uint256) {\n        return oracle.getTokenAmountByVToken(address(asset()), IERC20(address(this)).totalSupply(), Math.Rounding.Floor);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Convert assets to shares through the oracle\n    /// @dev In the current mainnet phase, 1:1 means `assets == shares`\n    function _convertToShares(uint256 assets, Math.Rounding rounding)\n        internal\n        view\n        virtual\n        override\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        return oracle.getVTokenAmountByToken(address(asset()), assets, rounding);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Convert shares to assets through the oracle\n    /// @dev In the current mainnet phase, 1:1 means `shares == assets`\n    function _convertToAssets(uint256 shares, Math.Rounding rounding)\n        internal\n        view\n        virtual\n        override\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        return oracle.getTokenAmountByVToken(address(asset()), shares, rounding);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Deposit assets and mint shares with INV-1 validation\n    /// @dev In the current mainnet phase (Oracle = 1:1), this path mints stPROS 1:1\n    function deposit(uint256 assets, address receiver)\n        public\n        virtual\n        override\n        whenNotPaused\n        checkInv1\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        uint256 vTokenAmount = super.deposit(assets, receiver);\n        return vTokenAmount;\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Mint a target number of shares with INV-1 validation\n    /// @dev In the current mainnet phase (Oracle = 1:1), this path back-solves assets 1:1\n    function mint(uint256 shares, address receiver)\n        public\n        virtual\n        override\n        whenNotPaused\n        checkInv1\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        uint256 tokenAmount = super.mint(shares, receiver);\n        return tokenAmount;\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Start an asset redemption by enqueuing it instead of paying immediately\n    /// @dev In the current mainnet phase (Oracle = 1:1), `assets` match the escrowed shares\n    function withdraw(uint256 assets, address receiver, address owner)\n        public\n        virtual\n        override\n        whenNotPaused\n        checkInv1\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        return super.withdraw(assets, receiver, owner);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Start a share redemption by enqueuing it instead of paying immediately\n    /// @dev In the current mainnet phase (Oracle = 1:1), `shares` map to equal `assets`\n    function redeem(uint256 shares, address receiver, address owner)\n        public\n        virtual\n        override\n        whenNotPaused\n        checkInv1\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        return super.redeem(shares, receiver, owner);\n    }\n\n    function _withdraw(address caller, address receiver, address owner, uint256 assets, uint256 shares)\n        internal\n        virtual\n        override\n    {\n        // Spend allowance first when the caller is acting on behalf of the owner.\n        if (caller != owner) {\n            _spendAllowance(owner, caller, shares);\n        }\n\n        // Move the user's shares into contract custody so they can be burned on completion.\n        _burn(owner, shares);\n        _mint(address(this), shares);\n\n        // O(1) enqueue: write at tail and advance the tail cursor.\n        uint256 head = withdrawalHead[owner];\n        uint256 tail = withdrawalTail[owner];\n        uint256 length = tail - head;\n\n        if (length >= maxWithdrawCount) {\n            revert ExceedMaxWithdrawCount(length);\n        }\n\n        withdrawals[owner][tail] = Withdrawal({\n            queued: queuedWithdrawal,\n            pending: assets,\n            createdAt: block.timestamp,\n            unbondingPeriod: unbondingPeriod\n        });\n        withdrawalTail[owner] = tail + 1;\n        // Count the request toward the reserve immediately; there is no external refill in the current phase.\n        queuedWithdrawal += assets;\n        totalCanWithdrawAmount += assets;\n\n        emit Withdraw(caller, receiver, owner, assets, shares);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Override ERC20 update logic to maintain INV-1 internal accounting\n    function _update(address from, address to, uint256 value) internal virtual override {\n        if (from == address(0)) {\n            _tracked += value;\n        }\n        if (to == address(0)) {\n            _tracked -= value;\n        }\n        super._update(from, to, value);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice INV-1: internal accounting must equal total stPROS supply\n    function _assertInv1() internal view {\n        uint256 stProsSupply = totalSupply();\n        uint256 trackedAmount = _tracked;\n        if (trackedAmount != stProsSupply) {\n            revert Inv1Violation(trackedAmount, stProsSupply);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @notice ERC165 interface declaration for IERC4626 + IERC20 + parent interfaces\n    function supportsInterface(bytes4 interfaceId) public view virtual override returns (bool) {\n        return interfaceId == type(IERC4626).interfaceId || interfaceId == type(IERC20).interfaceId\n            || super.supportsInterface(interfaceId);\n    }\n}","keccak256":"0x6df22d59aa2254698d96a99af646d1832d8c30f89cc3c7dd1d08cd62cdefc730","license":"GPL-3.0"}},"version":1}

PROSNotSent().notice: Thrown when PROS is not sent

UnbondingPeriodTooLong(uint256,uint256).notice: Waiting period exceeds the governance cap

UnbondingPeriodChanged(uint256,uint256).notice: Global waiting period update event for future requests only

MAX_UNBONDING().notice: Governance cap on the waiting period to avoid excessive lockups

canWithdrawalAmount(address).notice: Preview the currently claimable amount for an address with no record limit

canWithdrawalAmount(address,uint256).notice: Preview the claimable amount for an address with batched processing

completedWithdrawal().notice: Historical total completed amount, monotonically increasing

deposit(uint256,address).notice: Deposit assets and mint shares with INV-1 validation

getWithdrawals(address).notice: Return the current outstanding withdrawal records for an address in queue order

initialize(address,address,string,string.notice: Override initialize to include reentrancy guard (for new deployments)

maxWithdrawCount().notice: Maximum number of outstanding withdrawal records per address

mint(uint256,address).notice: Mint a target number of shares with INV-1 validation

oracle().notice: Rate oracle for token <-> vToken conversion

pause().notice: Pause pausable entry points, owner only

queuedWithdrawal().notice: Historical total queued amount, monotonically increasing

redeem(uint256,address,address).notice: Start a share redemption by enqueuing it instead of paying immediately

setMaxWithdrawCount(uint256).notice: Set the max number of queued records per address, owner only

setOracle(address).notice: Set the oracle address, owner only

setUnbondingPeriod(uint256).notice: Set the global waiting period, owner only

supportsInterface(bytes4).notice: ERC165 interface declaration for IERC4626 + IERC20 + parent interfaces

totalAssets().notice: ERC4626 total-assets view derived from the oracle and current supply

totalCanWithdrawAmount().notice: Reserve currently available for completion, decreases on claim

unbondingPeriod().notice: Global waiting period used for new withdrawal requests, in seconds

withdraw(uint256,address,address).notice: Start an asset redemption by enqueuing it instead of paying immediately

withdrawComplete().notice: Complete claimable withdrawals, processing all records by default

withdrawComplete(address).notice: Complete claimable withdrawals, processing all records by default

withdrawComplete(address,uint256).notice: Complete claimable withdrawals in batches to control single-call gas

withdrawComplete(uint256).notice: Complete claimable withdrawals in batches, paying the caller by default

withdrawalHead(address).notice: Withdraw queue head index per user (inclusive)

withdrawalTail(address).notice: Withdraw queue tail index per user (exclusive)
